title: podman assigned the broadcast address to a container
body:
### Issue Description

Podman assigned the network broadcast address to container on a custom podman network configured with an IP range.  As expected, TCP/IP connections to the container fail.

```
sudo podman inspect mycontainer
               "Networks": {
                    "mynet": {
                         "EndpointID": "",
                         "Gateway": "10.89.0.1",
                         "IPAddress": "10.89.0.255",
                         "IPPrefixLen": 24,
                         "IPv6Gateway": "",
                         "GlobalIPv6Address": "",
                         "GlobalIPv6PrefixLen": 0,
                         "MacAddress": "5e:56:44:76:48:2f",
                         "NetworkID": "mynet",
                         "DriverOpts": null,
                         "IPAMConfig": null,
                         "Links": null,
                         "Aliases": [
                              "e213f578330a"
                         ]
                    }
```

```
sudo podman network inspect mynet
[
     {
          "name": "mynet",
          "id": "06b8b732280a6e9f7ebd2c4214c23ab6bac27212b625b3180f7520882287365a",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2023-06-13T05:05:46.878388443Z",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1",
                    "lease_range": {
                         "start_ip": "10.89.0.129",
                         "end_ip": "10.89.0.255"
                    }
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create podman network with ansible via containers-podman-1.10.1:
```
    - name: Configure podman network
      containers.podman.podman_network:
        name: "mynet"
        subnet: "10.89.0.0/24"
        gateway: "10.89.0.1"
        ip_range: "10.89.0.128/25"
        disable_dns: false
        driver: bridge
        internal: false
        state: present
        recreate: false
```
2. Run container on podman network mynet

### Describe the results you received

Once in a while, a container on podman network mynet is assigned the broadcast address 10.89.0.255, which causes TCP/IP connections to that container to fail.

### Describe the results you expected

The broadcast address should be exempt from assignment, ie the lease_range should be [start_ip,end_ip).



### podman info output


comments.body: Yeah that is clearly a bug, I am surprised the kernel even allows this in the first place.
